text: AI is increasingly common in the workplace, bringing significant changes to how we work. Many routine tasks, like data entry and scheduling, can now be handled through automation, freeing up employees for more creative work. Productivity tools, such as smart email assistants and chatbots, help us complete tasks faster and with fewer errors. Importantly, AI isn't replacing most jobs entirely; instead, it encourages human-AI collaboration, where people and technology work together to achieve better results. This shift means some job roles will change, requiring workers to learn new skills like managing AI systems and interpreting their outputs, leading to a more efficient but evolving work environment.
